Level 2 Description: Enhances image features or detects boundaries in raster images using various filtering techniques. This includes smoothing, sharpening, edge detection, and frequency-based transformations.
Level 3 Description: Filters applied in the frequency domain (e.g., Fourier or wavelet transforms) to separate or enhance image components at different frequency bands.

Level 1 Description: Processes and analyzes remotely sensed imagery to enhance quality, extract features, classify land cover, and perform quantitative analysis.
Level 2 Description: Enhances image features or detects boundaries in raster images using various filtering techniques. This includes smoothing, sharpening, edge detection, and frequency-based transformations.
Level 3 Description: Advanced filters that reduce noise while preserving edges or textures, often used in remote sensing for speckle noise reduction. Examples include bilateral and anisotropic diffusion filters.
